reportMetadata.reportTitle: Privacy Policy Compliance Audit Report — PrivacyGPT Seed Data
reportMetadata.methodology: Fetched each company's privacy policy URL, extracted policy content via Firecrawl, evaluated against 10 compliance fields (F1-F10) mapped to seed data fields, and cross-referenced existing seed data.

executiveSummary.overallComplianceRate: 72% fully compliant, 5.5% need updates, 22% partially compliant
keyFindings: Anthropic's trainOnDataByDefault is incorrectly set to true in seed data — policy shows training is opt-IN, not default
HuggingFace's childrenDataPolicy states 13+ but policy indicates 16+ baseline
Follow-up audit (2026-06-25) re-fetched policies for 10 PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT companies — 4 elevated to COMPLIANT (cerebras, together-ai, replicate, sambanova), 6 remain PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT
Groq privacy policy explicitly excludes Cloud Services (GroqCloud/GroqChat/API) from scope — the Groq Services Agreement / DPA is the governing document

Remaining UNCLEAR fields: deepseek F7 (humanReviewOfChats), openrouter F4 (retentionPeriod) and F10 (enterpriseVsConsumerDifference), fireworks-ai F7 (humanReviewOfChats), cohere F7 (humanReviewOfChats), groq all F3-F10 (policy scope gap)
mlc.ai confirmed as open-source project with no privacy policy (hasValidPrivacyPolicy: false)

skippedCompanies.note: Open-source framework, no privacy policy applicable
Open-source research organization, no privacy policy

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy states 'we may use Content you provide us to improve our Services, for example to train the models' with opt-out available.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Consumer vs API/Enterprise difference explicitly addressed.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Opt-out via Settings > Data Controls.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: 30-day deletion for disabled training, de-identified retention for longer.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Deletion within 30 days confirmed.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: Vendors, service providers, affiliates enumerated.

F7_humanReviewOfChats.evidenceLine: Human review for safety/abuse monitoring confirmed.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: Separate EEA/UK/Switzerland policy with GDPR rights.

F9_childrenDataPolicy.evidenceLine: Minimum age 13+, under 18 needs parental consent.

F1_trainsOnDataByDefault.evidenceLine: Policy states 'We will use your chats...if: 1. You choose to allow us' — this is opt-IN, not default.

F2_trainingNuance.evidenceLine: Seed data says 'by default' but policy shows opt-in model.

F3_optOutAvailable.evidenceLine: Data controls available via Claude Settings.

F4_retentionPeriod.evidenceLine: 30-day retention if disabled, up to 5 years for de-identified research.

F5_dataDeletedOnRequest.evidenceLine: Deletion within 30 days.

F6_thirdPartySharing.evidenceLine: Hosting providers with strict DPAs.

F8_regionalVariation.evidenceLine: GDPR for EU/UK users.

correctiveActions.correctiveClause: Claude Free and Pro accounts do NOT train on your data by default. Model training requires affirmative opt-in consent from the user. API and Enterprise accounts remain excluded from training under all circumstances.

correctiveActions.oldValue: Consumer chats on Claude Free and Pro are used for model training by default. Console API and Enterprise plans are excluded from training.
correctiveActions.newValue: Consumer chats on Claude Free and Pro require user opt-in for model training. Console API and Enterprise plans are excluded from training.
correctiveActions.correctiveClause: Consumer Claude Free and Pro accounts require you to opt in before any chats are used for model improvement. Console API and Enterprise plans are excluded from training entirely.

correctiveActions.note: CRITICAL: Groq's privacy policy at groq.com/privacy explicitly states it does NOT apply to GroqCloud/GroqChat/API Customer Data. The governing document is the Groq Services Agreement / DPA. All UNCLEAR fields remain unresolved due to this scope gap.

recommendations: 1. Re-fetch privacy policies for companies with UNCLEAR fields: DeepSeek, OpenRouter, Groq, Cerebras, Fireworks AI, Together AI, Cohere, Replicate, SambaNova, MLC AI
2. For Chinese companies (DeepSeek, Z.ai, StepFun, Unsound, ByteDance), consider using a VPN/proxy to access policy URLs from a permitted region for more thorough verification
3. Set MLC AI's confidence to 'verified_from_policy_text' once a privacy policy is located (edge inference projects often lack traditional privacy policies)
4. Run 'bun run test' to verify schema constraints after applying seed data changes
5. Consider adding a 'needsReview' flag or automated re-verification reminder for companies with UNCLEAR fields
6. For proxy/gateway services (OpenRouter, Portkey, LiteLLM, Kong), consider that their privacy posture is inherently defined by their upstream provider's policies, which may change independently
